response:
"What would an innocent person say after he falsely received a verdict from the court?"

SECTION 1  
An innocent person, having been wrongly judged, might express confusion and disbelief. They could say, "This is not right. I didn’t do anything wrong." The emotional weight of the moment might lead them to question the fairness of the system. A silver lining, if any, might be the chance to prove their innocence. They might ask, "How could this happen?" as they try to wrap their mind around the injustice.

SECTION 2  
Sir, I didn’t commit the crime. I swear on my life. The lead investigator must have made a mistake. A stranger approached me earlier, claiming to know the truth. I didn’t know what to think. I just want to clear my name. I’ll do whatever it takes to prove my innocence.
